settings.title: What is fill power and why does it matter?
settings.answer: Fill power measures the loft and insulating ability of down. Higher fill power (like our 760-1000FP) means lighter weight, better compressibility, and superior warmth-to-weight ratio.

settings.title: How do I choose the right temperature rating?
settings.answer: Temperature ratings indicate the lowest comfortable temperature. Consider your metabolism, shelter type, and whether you sleep warm or cold when selecting your gear.

settings.title: What makes UDD technology special?
settings.answer: Our Ultra Dry Down treatment creates a water-resistant barrier on each feather, maintaining 90% insulation even when wet, compared to just 30% for untreated down.

settings.title: Where are NANGA products manufactured?
settings.answer: All NANGA products are designed and manufactured in our facility in Maibara, Shiga Prefecture, Japan, maintaining complete quality control throughout production.
